mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns exceed 1.5% in 70% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 240, or Rank IC > 0.15 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in PAYEMS and declining UNRATE signal tightening labor markets, increasing corporate margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows Rank IC < 0.05, or 5-day returns are negative in 60% of co-moving instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO signals reduced industrial output, increasing recession risks and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns exceed 3% in 50% of INDPRO decline periods, or Rank IC > 0.10 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates robust economic demand, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in 60% of GDPC1 growth periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong consumer confidence and demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 50% of HOUST > 1000 instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with low inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals sustainable demand growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between RSAFS and CPIAUCSL shows Rank IC < 0.05, or 20-day returns are negative in 50% of co-moving instances.

mechanism_narrative: High unemployment (UNRATE > 5%) signals weak labor market conditions, reducing corporate profits and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns exceed 2% in 60% of UNRATE > 5% periods, or Rank IC > 0.10 over 5-year rolling windows.
